domain: espalier fruit tree training (horticultural technique of training apple/pear/plum/peach into 2D planar form via wire-and-post trellis + annual pruning of out-of-plane shoots; 5-10 year commitment locks tree's branch schema)
specific_mechanism: A young tree is planted next to a horizontal-wire trellis; central leader is allowed but lateral branches are tied to the wires forming horizontal cordons. EVERY GROWING SEASON, all out-of-plane shoots are pruned, and in-plane shoots are tip-shortened. After 5-10 years the tree's growth has been disciplined into a fully 2D planar branch schema; the tree thereafter requires reduced maintenance but produces fruit only along the trellis form.
llm_application: Espalier-trellis adapter training: define a sparse TRELLIS pattern S in parameter coordinate space (authored offline as a structured selector — e.g., 'layers 8-12, heads 4-8 only, channel-band 256-512'). LoRA-style adapter is trainable ONLY at coordinates in S; gradients out-of-S are hard-zeroed before optimizer step. Property A: out-of-trellis weights cannot drift, so unlearned/safety-critical regions are protected from any fine-tune. Property B: multiple sequential fine-tunes accumulate adapter capacity ALONG the same trellis (no cross-region interference). Property C: trellis is a HUMAN-AUTHORED prior — interpretability + reproducibility. Property D: composable — distinct trellises for distinct skills can be stacked at deployment via mask union without coordinate collision.
novelty_claim: Structured pruning (LLM-Pruner 2305.11627, SlimLLM 2505.22689, BIP 2412.06419) prunes after training. Alignment-Constrained Dynamic Pruning (2511.07482) and RAP (2505.17138) constrain pruning dynamically. None proposes the espalier discipline: (i) human-authored offline trellis prior; (ii) hard zero of out-of-trellis gradients during training; (iii) sequential-fine-tune accumulation along same trellis; (iv) deployment-time compositional union of multiple trellis adapters. The trellis-as-authored-prior + training-time gradient discipline distinguishes from learned-mask sparsity (e.g., movement pruning, lottery tickets).
